headline: The next humanoid robot might not look human at all
summary: Genesis AI's Eno robot challenges traditional humanoid designs. It may lack heads, legs, or even operate on a wheeled base. The startup asserts humanoid robots don't need human aesthetics.

headline: The Complete Calvin and Hobbes is a great last-minute Father’s Day gift
summary: "The Complete Calvin and Hobbes" is recommended for Father's Day. It compiles every beloved strip from Bill Watterson's ten-year run. An ideal, easy last-minute gift for comic strip enthusiasts.

headline: All the latest news on Android 17, Wear OS 7, and Android XR
summary: Android 17 brings Bubble app windows, Screen Reaction, and split-gaming for foldables. Wear OS 7 features Live Updates, improved battery life, and XR smart glasses prep. Google rolls out significant updates across its mobile and wearable platforms.

headline: Android 17 arrives on Pixel phones today
summary: Android 17 is rolling out to compatible Pixel phones starting today. It includes additional exclusive features as part of the June Pixel Drop. Not all features announced previously are available immediately.

headline: Nintendo Acknowledges Employee Data at Risk After Third-Party Service Breach
summary: Nintendo confirms a data breach impacting employee information. The breach occurred via a third-party service. This follows earlier claims of the company's own systems being hacked.

headline: Ubisoft Acknowledges 'Community Didn't Resonate' With Assassin's Creed Shadows Story as Much as Hoped, While Fans Praise Final Update
summary: Ubisoft admits Assassin's Creed Shadows' story didn't fully resonate with fans. Players are praising the game's final update for fixing plot holes. The update reportedly provides a more satisfying ending to the game.

headline: Why Microsoft Announced Ninja Theory's New Game Despite Plans to Shut the Studio Down
summary: Microsoft revealed Ninja Theory's new game despite alleged plans for closure. The announcement aimed to attract investor interest in the studio. Game File reports Microsoft intended to close or spin off Ninja Theory.

headline: Next Batch of Free Cars Confirmed for Forza Horizon 6
summary: Forza Horizon 6 confirms a new batch of free cars. The 'Horizon Decades' playlist series runs from June 18 to July 16. Players can expect numerous new vehicles during this period.

headline: PM warns Burnham against immediate leadership challenge if he wins by-election
summary: Keir Starmer warns Andy Burnham against an immediate leadership challenge. This warning comes if Burnham wins the Makerfield by-election. Starmer suggests focusing on a subsequent Manchester mayoral race.

headline: Israel launches fresh strikes on Lebanon despite Trump criticism
summary: Israel has launched new strikes on Lebanon. This action follows criticism from Donald Trump. Trump urged PM Netanyahu to be more responsible regarding Lebanon.

headline: Inflation unexpectedly steady as food price rises slow
summary: Inflation remains unexpectedly steady, according to ONS data. Slower increases in food prices offset higher petrol costs. Price rises for meat, dairy, and vegetables have moderated.

headline: 'It's very Bond': Fashion experts on the England squad's off-pitch look
summary: Fashion experts analyze the England men's team's official off-duty attire. Their look is described as "very Bond" ahead of the World Cup. The team's style is garnering significant attention.
